stories.title: Rakdos Boggart returns as counter-meta
stories.summary: A near-Lorwyn-themed red-black goblin shell vanished for six weeks before the Pro Tour, then came back stronger than ever the week after.
stories.detail: The Boggart shell, anchored by Mudbutton Cursetosser, Boggart Cursecrafter, Searslicer Goblin and Stadium Headliner, first surfaced in March 2025 as a fringe pick at 4 shell cards per list. It locked in as a deeper 8-card package when Edge of Eternities reshuffled the format in February 2026, then went quiet from late March through April. The week after Pro Tour Final Fantasy, two separate Platinum-Mythic players brought it back with 8 and 9 shell cards each, the densest the package has ever been. A deliberate counter-pick against the post-PT field is the most parsimonious read.

timeline.note: fringe, 4 to 5 shell cards per appearance

stories.title: Erode jumps from 1 percent to 17 percent
stories.summary: An Erode-anchored counter-tempo shell did not exist on ladder before the Pro Tour. It is now in one in every six ladder winners.
stories.detail: Erode showed up on 27-04-2026 in a small number of lists at 3 percent share. The week after Pro Tour Final Fantasy it surged to 16.7 percent of ladder winners, on the back of a catalyst shell that includes Dyadrine, Hardened Academic, Flitterwing Nuisance, Shardmage's Rescue and Lumbering Worldwagon. It is the biggest single-card week-over-week jump in the dataset.

stories.title: Five Pro Tour staples the ladder has not caught
stories.summary: Colorstorm Stallion, Hearth Elemental, Tablet of Discovery, Prismari Charm and Impractical Joke are all over 5 percent at the Pro Tour and effectively 0 percent on ladder.
stories.detail: Pro Tour Final Fantasy showcased five cards that the ladder has not yet replicated at any meaningful share. Colorstorm Stallion at 20 percent of PT decks versus 0 percent on ladder is the most dramatic. Prismari Charm sits at 27 percent PT versus 0.4 percent ladder, a 64x ratio. The lag suggests these picks need either an infrastructure ladder has not yet built around, or that pros are taking risks the field has not vetted. In either case, expect at least two of the five to follow Erode's trajectory and break out in the next two weeks.
